description: DOM-based XSS: payload flows from a source to a sink entirely client-side, never touching the server.
approach: 1. Identify DOM sources: location.hash, location.search, location.pathname, document.referrer, window.name, postMessage
2. Trace flow in JS to DOM sinks

document_referrer.description: Input from HTTP Referrer header read via document.referrer
document_referrer.approach: Set Referer header to payload, or embed target in page with malicious link

window_name.description: Input from window.name (persists across same-origin navigations)
window_name.attack: Open target in new window: window.open(url); then set target.name = payload

postMessage.approach: Send crafted postMessage from attacker.com: window.postMessage('', '*')

testing_approach: 1. Review JS files for dangerous sink patterns (innerHTML, eval, document.write)
2. Trace backward from sink to find what variable is written to it
3. Trace forward from each source to find what reaches a sink
4. Craft payload based on source type and sink context
5. Test in Playwright — DOM XSS never leaves a server-side trace
